context_governance.rule: Report auto-load count and approximate token total at session start as part of acknowledgement
context_governance.condition: Always. Not a warning — a stated fact. Visible baseline for the session.
context_governance.auto_load_ceiling_note: Maximum auto-load chunks beyond session_loader. Currently using 1 (behaviour_personality). Soft ceiling — Claude warns, user decides, decision logged in versioning.
context_governance.session_chunk_ceiling_note: Maximum total chunks loaded in a single session. Soft ceiling — Claude warns, user decides. KB review sessions exempt.
context_governance.kb_review_exemption: Declaring session intent as KB review suspends the session chunk ceiling. Auto-load ceiling still applies. Lock protocol still available.
context_governance.self_monitoring_limit: Claude cannot reliably detect its own reasoning degradation mid-session. Context monitoring is structural — chunk counts and sizes at load time — not qualitative.

builtin_memory_boundary.rule: Claude's built-in memory and MemoryAccess must not overlap.
builtin_memory_allowed: Truly personal context unrelated to any project
builtin_memory_forbidden: Architectural decisions or technical preferences tied to a project
Summaries or approximations of content already in MemoryAccess
Open questions, roadmap items, findings, or framework descriptions
builtin_memory_boundary.enforcement: At session end, review new built-in memory entries and flag any that violate the boundary. Present to user for confirmation before deletion.
builtin_memory_boundary.condition: Applies whenever built-in memory is active.

lock_protocol.trigger: User reports erratic behavior or unexpected output quality
lock_protocol.response: Acknowledge, stop all new chunk fetches for remainder of session, note current loaded chunks and session position, complete current work on what is in context, record lock event in meta.session_lock at session end
lock_protocol.purpose: Data collection — builds evidence for ceiling calibration over time.
lock_protocol.kb_review_note: Lock protocol remains available during KB review sessions.
